zh.lifeSummary: Agnes Jan Macharius van den Bossche, van Gent於大約1285年出生在荷蘭努-布拉班特也-黑達歐根包斯。她和Ridder en Heer van Nuland Albert Jan Heimerik van Dordrecht至少有3個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於1309年之後在她的家鄉。

zh-hans.'s-Hertogenbosch, s-Hertogenbosch, North: 荷兰努-布拉班特也-黑達歐根包斯
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Agnes Jan Macharius van den Bossche, van Gent于大约1285年出生在荷兰努-布拉班特也-黑達歐根包斯。她和Ridder en Heer van Nuland Albert Jan Heimerik van Dordrecht至少有3个儿子和1个女儿。她去世于1309年之后在她的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: den
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 鄧, see   Deng  .
Chinese: possibly from the Romanization of certain Chinese personal names of some early Chinese immigrants in the US.

Origin undetermined. — Note: It is possible that in the population figure published by the US Census Bureau the Dutch case-inflected definite article de ‘the’, which forms first part of surnames such as Den Bleyker and Den Boer , is in some instances counted as an independent surname. Compare Ten .

variants.name: Deng
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Hua, Jian, Jing, Wei, Wen, Yi, Ying, Chao, Fang, Gang, Hui, Min.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 鄧: (i) from Deng (鄧), the name of a state (located in Dengxian in Henan province) granted to Man Ji, uncle of Wu Ding (a king of the Shang dynasty (1600 –1046 BC )). (ii) from Deng Bowen (鄧伯溫), head of an ancient state (located in present-day Shandong province) dating back to the reign of the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (iii) from Deng (鄧), a fief (located in Hubei province) granted to descendants of Zhong Kang, the 4th king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600 BC ). (iv) from Deng Cheng (鄧城, City of Deng), a town in the state of Cai (located in present-day Henan province) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). (v) from Deng (鄧), a title of Li Tianyi, the 8th son of Li Yu (the last king of the state of Southern Tang (937–975 AD ) during the Five Dynasties and Ten Kingdoms (907–960) periods). The state of Southern Tang was annexed by Emperor Taizong of Song (939–997 AD ) in 975 AD , and the royal families of South Tang were hunted down. Li Tianyi's son fled and changed the surname (Li) to Deng (鄧), his father's title.

nameMeanings.name: Bossche,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Dutch: variant of   Busscher   ‘lumberjack’. Compare   Bosker  .

variants.name: Busscher
meanings: Dutch: occupational name for a lumberjack, from bus(s)cher, an agent derivative of busch ‘wood’. Compare Buscher .
German (Büsscher): variant of Büscher (see Buscher 1).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Gent
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern) and French: nickname from Old French gent, Middle English gent(e) ‘high born, noble’, hence ‘noble of character or conduct, courteous’, also ‘nice, friendly’.
English: habitational name from Ghent (Gent in Dutch), a city in Flanders, Belgium. Compare Ghent .
German: from a short form of an ancient Germanic personal name formed with gand ‘spell’, such as Gandolf.

variants.name: Jent
meanings: English: variant of   Gent  .

variants.name: Ghent
meanings: English: habitational name from Ghent (Gent in Dutch), a city in Flanders, Belgium. Compare Gent .

variants.name: Gentes
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Henri, Lucien, Marcel, Normand.
French (Gentès): probably a nickname derived from Old French gent ‘high born, noble’, also ‘nice, friendly’.
German: patronymic from the personal name   Gent  .

variants.name: Genther
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Otto.
German:
variant of   Gent  .

variants.name: Genthe
meanings: German: variant of Gente (see Gent ), or a habitational name from Gentha in Saxony-Anhalt, east of Wittenberg.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

nameMeanings.name: Jan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

variants.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

en.lifeSummary: Agnes Jan Macharius van den Bossche, van Gent was born about 1285, in 's-Hertogenbosch, North Brabant, Netherlands. She had at least 3 sons and 1 daughter with Ridder en Heer van Nuland Albert Jan Heimerik van Dordrecht. She died after 1309, in her hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Agnes Jan Macharius van den Bossche, van Gent est née environ 1285, à 's-Hertogenbosch, Brabant-septentrional, Pays-Bas. Elle a eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille avec Ridder en Heer van Nuland Albert Jan Heimerik van Dordrecht. Elle est décédée après 1309, dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Agnes Jan Macharius van den Bossche, van Gent wurde um 1285 in 's-Hertogenbosch, Nord-Brabant, Niederlande geboren. Sie hatte mit Ridder en Heer van Nuland Albert Jan Heimerik van Dordrecht mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb nach 1309 in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Agnes Jan Macharius van den Bossche, van Gent è nata il circa 1285 nel 's-Hertogenbosch, Brabante settentrionale, Paesi Bassi. Lei avuto almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia con Ridder en Heer van Nuland Albert Jan Heimerik van Dordrecht. Morì dopo il 1309, nel sua cittadina.

ja.'s-Hertogenbosch, s-Hertogenbosch, North: オランダノオルド-ブラバント'ス-ハートジェンボッシュ

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes Jan Macharius van den Bossche, van Gentは約1285年にオランダノオルド-ブラバント'ス-ハートジェンボッシュで生まれた。彼女はRidder en Heer van Nuland Albert Jan Heimerik van Dordrechtとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1309年以後に故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Jan Macharius van den Bossche, van Gent(은)는 대략 1285년 네덜란드 노르드-브라반트 '스-헤르토겐보슈에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Ridder en Heer van Nuland Albert Jan Heimerik van Dordrecht(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1309년 이후 그녀의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.Lith, Lith, North Brabant, Netherlands: Lith, Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos
pt.lifeSummary: Agnes Jan Macharius van den Bossche, van Gent nasceu aproximadamente 1285, em 's-Hertogenbosch, Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos. Ela teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha com Ridder en Heer van Nuland Albert Jan Heimerik van Dordrecht. Ela faleceu após 1309, em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Agnes Jan Macharius van den Bossche, van Gent родилась около 1285 г. в местности 'с-хиртодженбощ, нурд-брабант, Нидерланды. У неё было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь от Ridder en Heer van Nuland Albert Jan Heimerik van Dordrecht. Она умерла после 1309 г. в её родном городе.

sm.Abt 1310-1320: mai le e tusa o le 1310 e o'o I le 1320

es.lifeSummary: Agnes Jan Macharius van den Bossche, van Gent nació aproximadamente 1285, en 's-Hertogenbosch, Brabante del Norte, Países Bajos. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija con Ridder en Heer van Nuland Albert Jan Heimerik van Dordrecht. Murió después de 1309, en su pueblo.
